title: Spot SQL injection in PHP query string concatenation
prompt: Review the file `vulnerable.php` for security issues. Walk through the code and call out anything that could be exploited.

fixtures.vulnerable.php:
<?php
// Simple user lookup endpoint.
require_once 'db.php';

function connect() {
    return new mysqli('localhost', 'app', 'app', 'shop');
}

function lookup_user($username) {
    $db = connect();
    $sql = "SELECT id, email, role FROM users WHERE username = '" . $username . "'";
    $result = $db->query($sql);
    if (!$result) {
        return null;
    }
    return $result->fetch_assoc();
}

function render($user) {
    if ($user === null) {
        echo 'no such user';
        return;
    }
    echo 'Hello ' . $user['email'];
}

$username = $_GET['u'] ?? '';
$user = lookup_user($username);
render($user);
